title: helianthella microcephala
intro: Helianthella microcephala, also known as fringed prairie clover, is a perennial plant native to the Great Plains region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds and reduce inflammation. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are said to have health benefits including reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties are believed to help prevent infection and promote tissue repair.
